title: [MTR4] 209系・E501系|JR East 209/E501 series
description: Resource pack that adds JR 209/E501 Series to Minecraft via Minecraft Transit Railway.
body:
_A resource pack for Minecraft Transit Railway._

# Overview
The **JR 209-0 Series** are trains produced by Kawasaki, Tokyu Car Corporation, and JR Niitsu Rolling Stock Plant between 1992 and 1998. A total of 812 vehicles are produced for the Keihin–Tōhoku—Negishi Line and the Nambu Line, to replace the 103 series running on both lines. The concept of these trains are to reduce the cost of building the train, reducing the weight of the train, and reducing the lifespan of the train, and the train has become the basis for other trains such as E501, E217, 701 and E127 series, and the E231 series. In 2007 trains running on the Keihin–Tōhoku—Negishi Line are replaced by the E233-1000 series as part of the concept of 209 series, however most of the front/rear coaches and some intermediate coaches were saved for scrapping, as they're to be repurposed for training, or to be converted to the **209-2000/2100 series** that serves Sōbu Main Line, Sotobō, Tōgane, Uchibō and Narita Lines. Rural services on these lines are replaced by E131 series and 2 sets of them has been written off and transferred to Izukyu and reclassified as the "3000 series". 

The **JR E501 Series** are trains produced by Kawasaki and Tokyu Car Corporation between 1995 and 1997. A total of 60 vehicles are produced for the Jōban and Mito Lines. These trains are equipped with dual-voltage equipment in order to operate services beyond Toride, as AC power must be used  to avoid interference with the Japan Meteorological Agency's Magnetic Observatory in Ishioka City. These trains are to be replaced by E531 series, and some of these trains are rumoured to be transferred to JR Kyushu.



This resource pack is to add the JR 209-2000/2100 series and E501 series into Minecraft with Minecraft Transit Railway mod, made by Navi_LX9702 in 2025.

# Disclaimer

This resource pack is not associated with, and is not an official product of, East Japan Railway Company.
